title: Regular Assessment of Licensed Recruitment Agencies
content:
SECTION 35. Scope of Regular Assessment. - The Assessment is required for upgrading provisional licenses to regular licenses, renewal of licenses and branch authority, transfer of office, and biennially after license renewal. It covers: (a) maintenance of minimum office space, facilities, and equipment; (b) maintenance of required signs and postings including POEA door sticker, agency license, organizational chart, 'Kaukulang Bayad' poster, and anti-illegal recruitment campaign posters; (c) examination of books of accounts, official receipts, and financial statements; and (d) compliance with general labor standards and occupational safety and health standards.

SECTION 36. Issuance of Authority to Assess. - The LRO Director or authorized representative issues authority to assess to duly authorized LLCOs, specifying LLCO names, agency to be assessed, purpose, and validity period. The LRO Director may also request the DOLE Regional Director to conduct assessments including branches.

SECTION 37. Conduct and Report of Assessment. - The LLCO presents authority to the agency, which must be received and signed by a responsible agency representative. Assessment is conducted in the presence of the agency representative and a rank and file employee representative, following the Assessment Checklist in Annex 'A'.

SECTION 38. Issuance of Notice of Results. - The LLCO issues a Notice of Results indicating compliance or deficiencies with POEA rules, labor laws, and social legislation, acknowledged by the agency representative.

SECTION 39. Report of Assessment. - The LLCO endorses results related to overseas employment to the LRO Director, and those related to general labor standards and occupational safety and health to the DOLE Regional Director.

SECTION 40. Issuance of Certificate of Compliance. - Agencies complying with overseas employment rules receive a Certificate of Compliance valid for two years from the POEA Administrator within five days of compliance. Certificates for general labor standards and occupational safety and health are issued by the DOLE Regional Director. Certificates cover branch offices included in license renewal.

SECTION 41. Compliance with Deficiencies. - For non-compliance, the LLCO issues a Notice of Results with the assessment checklist indicating deficiencies. Agencies must comply as follows:
(a) Deficiencies in office facilities, equipment, postings, and signs must be corrected within five working days with proof submitted; failure results in suspension of documentary processing until compliance.
(b) Failure to maintain minimum office space requires proof of compliance within 30 days; failure results in suspension of documentary processing until compliance.
(c) If audited financial statements show capitalization below required minimum, additional capital must be infused within 30 days with SEC certification; failure results in license suspension until compliance.
(d) Non-presentation of books of accounts and official receipts during assessment allows 48 hours to present them; failure results in suspension of documentary processing.
(e) Presence of unauthorized persons on premises is reported for administrative proceedings.
Deficiencies in general labor standards and occupational safety and health are under DOLE jurisdiction per LLCS Rules.
